chunks.text: Stereo. H C J D A 38 Judgment Sheet IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, LAHORE (JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT) Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 Abu Ammar Rashid versus The State etc. @ Gullu, etc. Capital Sentence Reference No.21-T of 2016 The State versus Abu Ammar Rashid @ Gullu, etc. Crl. Appeal No.2102 of 2016 Muhammad Ashraf versus Shahbaz @ Tipu, etc. Crl. Revision No.1998 of 2016 Muhammad Ashraf versus Abu Ammar Rashid, etc. Date of hearing 19.11.2019 Appellants Abu-Ammar Rashid and M/s Saeed Ullah Khan, Saleem Hamza alias Hamza by Rana Baleegh-ur- Rehman and Muhammad Zubair Sahi, Advocates. Appellant Waqas @ Babban by Azam Nazir Tarar and Mazhar Ali Ghillu, Advocates The Complainant by Mr. Waqar Hassan Mir, Advocate. Respondents No.1 & 3 (Crl. M/s Sher Gul Qureshi and Appeal No.2102 of 2016) by Mehram Ali Bali, Advocates respectively. The State by Mr. Muhammad Moeen Ali, Deputy Prosecutor General ======= Asjad Javaid Ghural, J. We propose to decide the above captioned matters through this common judgment arising out of impugned judgment dated 20.10.2016 passed by the learned Judge, Anti-Terrorism Court-I, Lahore in case FIR No.247 dated 26.03.2014, in respect of offences Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 2 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 under Sections 302, 324, 148, 149, 337-A(i), & 337-F(iii) PPC and Section 7 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 registered at Police Station, Harbanspura, Lahore. Through Criminal Appeal No.1914 of 2016 under Section 25 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 appellants Abu Ammar Rashid, Waqar and Saleem Shahzad alias Hamza have challenged their conviction and sentence awarded to them as under:- Under Section 302(b)/34 PPC Death on four counts each with compensation of Rs.50,000/- each to the legal heirs of deceased Muhammad Umer, Rafaqat Ali, Ali Raza and Muhammad Akram u/s 544-A Cr.P.C. and in default thereof to further undergo simple imprisonment for six months each.

Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 2 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 under Sections 302, 324, 148, 149, 337-A(i), & 337-F(iii) PPC and Section 7 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 registered at Police Station, Harbanspura, Lahore. Through Criminal Appeal No.1914 of 2016 under Section 25 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 appellants Abu Ammar Rashid, Waqar and Saleem Shahzad alias Hamza have challenged their conviction and sentence awarded to them as under:- Under Section 302(b)/34 PPC Death on four counts each with compensation of Rs.50,000/- each to the legal heirs of deceased Muhammad Umer, Rafaqat Ali, Ali Raza and Muhammad Akram u/s 544-A Cr.P.C. and in default thereof to further undergo simple imprisonment for six months each. Under Section 7(a) of Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 read with Section 34 PPC. Death on four counts each with compensation of Rs.100,000/- each to the legal heirs of deceased Muhammad Umer, Rafaqat Ali, Ali Raza and Muhammad Akram under Section 544-A Cr.P.C. and in default thereof to further undergo simple imprisonment for six months each. Under Section 324/34 PPC Rigorous Imprisonment for ten years on two counts each with fine of Rs.50,000/- each and in default thereof, to further undergo simple imprisonment for six months each. Under Section 7(b) of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 read with Section 34 PPC. Rigorous Imprisonment for ten years on two counts each with fine of Rs.50,000/- each and in default thereof, to further undergo simple imprisonment for six months each. Under Section 7(c) of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 read with Section 34 PPC. Rigorous Imprisonment for fourteen years on two counts each with fine of Rs.30,000/- each and in default thereof, to further undergo simple imprisonment for six months each. Under Section 337-F(iii)/34 PPC. Rigorous Imprisonment for two years and to pay Daman amount of Rs.20,000/- each to the injured. Under Section 337-A(i)/34 PPC. Rigorous Imprisonment for two years each and to pay Daman amount of Rs.20,000/- each to the injured. The benefit of Section 382-B PPC was extended to the convicts and their sentences were ordered to run concurrently. Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 3 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 2. Capital Sentence Reference No.21-T of 2016 was sent up by the learned trial Court for confirmation or otherwise of death sentence of convicts Abu Ammar Rashid alias Gullu, Waqas alias Babban and Saleem Shahzad alias Hamza.

Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 3 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 2. Capital Sentence Reference No.21-T of 2016 was sent up by the learned trial Court for confirmation or otherwise of death sentence of convicts Abu Ammar Rashid alias Gullu, Waqas alias Babban and Saleem Shahzad alias Hamza. Through Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2016 complainant Muhammad Ashraf has challenged the acquittal of respondents No.1 to 4 wherein, after hearing preliminary arguments, notices were issued to respondents No.1 & 3 (hereinafter shall be referred to as the respondents) and Crl. Revision No.1998 of 2016 the complainant has sought enhancement of sentence of respondents No.1 to 3. 3. The prosecution story unfolded in crime report (Ex.PA) registered on the complaint of Muhammad Ashraf (PW-5) is that he alongwith his father and brothers used to deal with the business of poultry farm, his sister namely, Mst. Shabana Bibi was married with her “khala zaad” Ijaz, who was residing at Fateh Garh, Lahore. Mst. Saira, a “Nand” of his sister contracted love-marriage with one Jehangir and got transferred the house of her father in her name through fraudulent means wherein she was residing with her family. When father-in-law of his sister came to know about this fraud, he obtained interim injunction from the Civil Court. On 25.03.2014, at about 9:30 p.m. Mst. Saira Bibi, her husband Jehangir, his brother Saleem Shehzad @ Hamza, Shehbaz @ Tipu, Yousaf Ameen and four unknown persons armed with fire arm weapons came there on a white car No.4877/LEA and two motorcycles in order to take forcible possession of the said house. Mst. Shabana informed her father about the situation, who alongwith the complainant and his other sons namely, Muhammad Akram, Rafaqat and Ali Raza went to Fateh Garh, Lahore for compromise. Binyameen, Muhammad Awais, his brother-in-law Ijaz and other relatives were already present there. They tried to patch up the matter but in the meanwhile, Shehbaz @ Tipu and Mst.

Binyameen, Muhammad Awais, his brother-in-law Ijaz and other relatives were already present there. They tried to patch up the matter but in the meanwhile, Shehbaz @ Tipu and Mst. Saira raised Lalkara that they did not accept them as “Panchaiti” and signaled their gunmen to teach them a lesson for not vacating the house. Saleem Shehzad @ Hamza, Shehbaz @ Tipu, Yousaf Ameen and four unknown accused, who could be identified when come across, started making indiscriminate firing towards them as a result of which Muhammad Umar, father and three brothers namely, Muhammad Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 4 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 Akram, Muhammad Rafqat and Ali Raza and cousins namely, Kashif and Kamran of the complainant received fire arm injuries. Accused Muhammad Jehangir and Saleem Shehzad also sustained fire arm injuries at the hands of their companions. The act of accused created the sense of fear and insecurity amongst the public at large. All the injured were shifted to Shalamar Hospital, Lahore but his father and brothers and one of the accused namely, Jehangir succumbed to the injuries in the way. 4. Syed Imtiaz Hussain Shah, Inspector (PW-25) visited the hospitals and took all necessary steps of initial investigation and transmitted the dead bodies of the deceased to the mortuary. He visited the place of occurrence on the same day and after completion of spot inspection and securing blood stained earth and collecting empties of different bores alongwith live bullets recorded the statements of witnesses under Section 161 Cr.P.C. On 28.03.2014 the complainant nominated accused Abu Ammar Rashid and Waqas through supplementary statement. He arrested accused Muhammad Saleem Shahzad on 31.03.2014 and Abu Ammar Rashid on 01.04.2014 and got conducted their test identification parade on 02.04.2014.

On 28.03.2014 the complainant nominated accused Abu Ammar Rashid and Waqas through supplementary statement. He arrested accused Muhammad Saleem Shahzad on 31.03.2014 and Abu Ammar Rashid on 01.04.2014 and got conducted their test identification parade on 02.04.2014. During investigation conducted by Muhammad Anwar, Inspector (PW-26), appellant Saleem Shahzad led to the recovery of pistol 9-MM alongwith five live bullets (P-12 & P-13/1-5) on 11.04.2014. He arrested appellant Muhammad Waqas alias Babban on 15.04.2014, who after test identification parade, led to the recovery of rifle 222-bore on 29.04.2014. On 20.04.2014 appellant Abu Ammar Rashid led to the recovery of rifle 7.62 alongwith 25-bullets vide seizer memo Ex.PJ/1. He recorded the statements of witnesses under Section 161 Cr.P.C. and after completion of investigation, got prepared report under Section 173 Cr.P.C. 5. Dr. Riasat Ali (PW-14) held autopsy on the dead body of deceased Ali Raza on 26.03.2014 at about 11:30 a.m. and observed five injuries on his person, which were ante-mortem in nature. Injuries No.1, 2 & 3 were caused by firearms and injuries No.4 & 5 were caused by blunt means. The cause of death was firearm trauma to the chest, neck and head under injury No.1, 2 & 3 respectively. The probable duration between injuries and death Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 5 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 was immediate whereas between death and postmortem examination it was within 14-15 hours. Dr. Muhammad Iqbal Ghani (PW-16) held autopsy on the dead body of deceased Rafaqat Ali on 26.04.2014 at 11:30 a.m. and observed two lacerated wounds on his body. Injury No.1(a+b) was caused by firearm weapons while the rest were the result of blunt means. The cause of death was injury to the vital organs in the chest and abdomen leading to hemorrhage shock and death under injury No.1(a+b), which was fatal and sufficient to cause death in ordinary course of nature.

Injury No.1(a+b) was caused by firearm weapons while the rest were the result of blunt means. The cause of death was injury to the vital organs in the chest and abdomen leading to hemorrhage shock and death under injury No.1(a+b), which was fatal and sufficient to cause death in ordinary course of nature. The probable duration between injuries and death was within few minutes whereas between death and postmortem examination within 14-15 hours. Dr. Muhammad Akmal (PW-17) conducted postmortem examination on the dead body of deceased Muhammad Umer on 26.03.2014 at 11:30 a.m. and observed two ante-mortem lacerated wounds on his body caused by firearm weapon. The cause of death was injury No.1(a+b) damage to level and abdominal aorta leading to hemorrhage shock and death was sufficient to cause death in ordinary course of nature. The probable duration between injuries and death was immediate whereas between death and postmortem examination within 14-15 hours. Dr. Syed Sohail Ahmed (PW-21) held autopsy on the dead body of deceased Muhammad Akram on 26.03.2014 at 11:30 a.m. and observed two lacerated wounds, which were ante-mortem caused by firearm causing damage to both lungs, liver, hepatic vessels leading to hemorrhage shock and death was sufficient to cause death of an individual in ordinary course of nature. The probable duration between injuries and death was 30-45 minutes and between death and postmortem was within 14-15 hours. 6. Dr. Tanveer Hussain (PW-15) conducted medico-legal examination of injured Muhammad Jahangir on 25.03.2014 and observed three firearm wounds on his body. On the same day, he conducted medico-legal examination of injured Kamran Shafique and observed a lacerated wound above and outer to left eye, which was kept under observation for surgical notes and X-rays. He further conducted medico-legal examination of injured Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 6 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 Kashif and observed two firearm injuries on the right outer buttock and on the back of left buttock. 7.

He further conducted medico-legal examination of injured Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 6 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 Kashif and observed two firearm injuries on the right outer buttock and on the back of left buttock. 7. At the commencement of the trial, trial Court framed charge against the appellants to which they pleaded not guilty and claimed to be tried. 8. The prosecution examined 26-witnesses besides the reports of Punjab Forensic Science Agency (Ex.PSS to Ex.PTT). The appellants in their statements recorded under Section 342 Cr.P.C., had denied and controverted all the allegations of fact leveled against them. They did not opt to make statements under Section 340 Cr.P.C. however, tendered various documents in their defence. 9. Learned trial Court, upon conclusion of the trial, convicted and sentenced the appellants, as stated above vide impugned judgment dated 20.10.2016. Hence, these criminal appeals as well as the connected Capital Sentence Reference and Criminal Revision. 10. Learned counsels for the appellants submit that the appellants are quite innocent and they have nothing to do with this occurrence; that appellants Waqas and Abu Ammar were not named in the crime report, who were implicated by the complainant in this case subsequently on 28.03.2014 without disclosing his source of information; that after their nomination by the complainant in this case, the exercise of test identification parade has lost its legal significance especially when no facial features of the unknown accused were described by any of the eye witnesses so as to identify them at some subsequent stage; that the prosecution witnesses have contradictory view with regard to nomination of the said accused; that the complainant assigned the joint role of making fire shots to the appellants alongwith respondents No.1 & 3, whereas, the injured witnesses had specifically ascribed injuries to each individual, which tantamount to dishonest improvement; that the medical evidence does not lend support to the ocular account observing some of the injuries caused by blunt means.

Hence, these criminal appeals as well as the connected Capital Sentence Reference and Criminal Revision. 10. Learned counsels for the appellants submit that the appellants are quite innocent and they have nothing to do with this occurrence; that appellants Waqas and Abu Ammar were not named in the crime report, who were implicated by the complainant in this case subsequently on 28.03.2014 without disclosing his source of information; that after their nomination by the complainant in this case, the exercise of test identification parade has lost its legal significance especially when no facial features of the unknown accused were described by any of the eye witnesses so as to identify them at some subsequent stage; that the prosecution witnesses have contradictory view with regard to nomination of the said accused; that the complainant assigned the joint role of making fire shots to the appellants alongwith respondents No.1 & 3, whereas, the injured witnesses had specifically ascribed injuries to each individual, which tantamount to dishonest improvement; that the medical evidence does not lend support to the ocular account observing some of the injuries caused by blunt means. On the basis of these contentions, learned counsels have prayed for acquittal of the appellants. 11. Learned counsel for respondents No.1 & 3 while defending impugned judgment to their extent submits that respondent Shehbaz @ Tipu was Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 7 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 alleged to have made aerial firing, whereas, respondent Yousaf Amin was attributed the role of making fire shot at the right flank of Muhammad Rafaqat (deceased) which was found non-existent in the medical evidence; that the trial Court had rightly extended the benefit of doubt to them which does not warrant interference by this Court. He has prayed for dismissal of appeal against the respondents. 12. Conversely, learned Deputy Prosecutor General appearing for the State assisted by learned counsel for the complainant has emphatically argued that the place of occurrence was outside house of the complainant’s sister namely, Mst. Shabana; that in our society, visit of a father and brothers at the house of daughter/sister on emergency call is not unusual; that the complainant’s father and three real brothers were murdered on the dispute of residential house; that no doubt appellants Abu Ammar and Waqas were nominated through supplementary statement but in cross-examination, the defence did not put any question to the complainant or the eye-witnesses regarding source of information as such the defence could not claim any benefit on that score; that the presence of eye-witnesses at the spot, who had sustained fire arm injuries, cannot be doubted; that the appellants and the acquitted respondents were equally liable for the murders in issue and also causing fire arm injuries to the injured witnesses; that the ocular account is in consonance with the medical evidence; that the weapons recovered from the appellants have been found wedded with crime empties secured from the place of occurrence and the report of the Punjab Forensic Agency, Lahore in that regard has been received with positive result; that motive behind the occurrence has not been denied rather admitted by the defence in so many words.

Conversely, learned Deputy Prosecutor General appearing for the State assisted by learned counsel for the complainant has emphatically argued that the place of occurrence was outside house of the complainant’s sister namely, Mst. Shabana; that in our society, visit of a father and brothers at the house of daughter/sister on emergency call is not unusual; that the complainant’s father and three real brothers were murdered on the dispute of residential house; that no doubt appellants Abu Ammar and Waqas were nominated through supplementary statement but in cross-examination, the defence did not put any question to the complainant or the eye-witnesses regarding source of information as such the defence could not claim any benefit on that score; that the presence of eye-witnesses at the spot, who had sustained fire arm injuries, cannot be doubted; that the appellants and the acquitted respondents were equally liable for the murders in issue and also causing fire arm injuries to the injured witnesses; that the ocular account is in consonance with the medical evidence; that the weapons recovered from the appellants have been found wedded with crime empties secured from the place of occurrence and the report of the Punjab Forensic Agency, Lahore in that regard has been received with positive result; that motive behind the occurrence has not been denied rather admitted by the defence in so many words. At the end, a prayer has been made for dismissal of appeal against conviction, acceptance of appeal against acquittal and revision for enhancement of sentence. 13. We have heard learned counsel for the appellants, learned counsel for respondents No.1 & 3, learned Deputy Prosecutor General appearing for the State assisted by learned counsel for the complainant and perused the record with their able assistance. Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 8 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 14. This gruesome incident took the lives of five persons, which had taken place on 25.03.2014 at 09:30 p.m. in the area of Fateh Garh Harbanspura, Lahore. Muhammad Ashraf (PW-5)/complainant, while appearing in the dock of court room deposed that his sister Mst. Shabana Bibi was married with her “khala zaad” Ijaz, a resident of Fateh Garh, Lahore. Mst. Saira “Nand” of his sister contracted love marriage with one Jehangir and got transferred the house of her father in her name allegedly through fraudulent means in which his sister was residing with her family. On coming to know about this fraud, father-in-law of his sister obtained interim injunction from the civil Court. On 25.03.2014, Mst. Saira Bibi, her husband Jehangir, his brother Saleem Shehzad @ Hamza alongwith their gunmen namely, Shehbaz @ Tipu, Yousaf Ameen and four unknown accused armed with fire arms came to that house on white colour car and two motorcycles with the intention to take forcible possession of the said house, his sister Mst. Shabana informed her father about the situation, who alongwith the complainant and other sons namely, Muhammad Akram, Rafaqat and Ali Raza went to Fateh Garh, Lahore for compromise.

Saira Bibi, her husband Jehangir, his brother Saleem Shehzad @ Hamza alongwith their gunmen namely, Shehbaz @ Tipu, Yousaf Ameen and four unknown accused armed with fire arms came to that house on white colour car and two motorcycles with the intention to take forcible possession of the said house, his sister Mst. Shabana informed her father about the situation, who alongwith the complainant and other sons namely, Muhammad Akram, Rafaqat and Ali Raza went to Fateh Garh, Lahore for compromise. Their relatives Binyameen, Muhammad Awais, his brother-in-law Ijaz and other relatives were already present there, they tried to patch up the matter but in the meanwhile, Shehbaz and Saira raised Lalkara that they did not accept them as “Panchaiti” and signaled their gunmen to teach them lesson for not vacating the house. Saleem Shehzad @ Hamza, Shehbaz @ Tipu, Yousaf Ameen, Waqas @ Baban and Abu Ammar Rashid started making indiscriminate firing at them. They ran hither and thither in order to save them but due to said firing his father Muhammad Umer, three brothers Muhammad Akram, Muhammad Rafqat and Ali Raza and cousins Kashif and Kamran sustained fire arm injuries. Accused Muhammad Jehangir and Saleem Shehzad also sustained injuries due to firing of their companions. The injured were shifted to Shalimar Hospital, Lahore but his father and brothers succumbed to the injuries in the way. Accused Muhammad Jehangir also lost his life in the incident. On 09.04.2014, he and other eye witnesses namely, Binyamin, Kamran and Kashif correctly identified accused Abu Ammar Rashid and further on 21.04.2014, they identified Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 9 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 appellant Waqas alias Baban in the test identification parade respectively at Camp Jail, Lahore. Muhammad Kashif (PW-6) and Kamran Shafique (PW-7) also made identical statement in line with the complainant while explaining the role of each individual accused. Learned counsels for the appellants have laid a lot of emphasis that assigning the role to each individual is in fact a dishonest improvement on the part of the injured PWs.

Muhammad Kashif (PW-6) and Kamran Shafique (PW-7) also made identical statement in line with the complainant while explaining the role of each individual accused. Learned counsels for the appellants have laid a lot of emphasis that assigning the role to each individual is in fact a dishonest improvement on the part of the injured PWs. We regret to disagree with this submission because Allah Almighty has blessed every human with different power of observation to visualize any situation with his/her own angle. Assigning of joint role by the complainant but distinguishing the role of each individual accused by the eye-witnesses in no manner can be construed as dishonest improvement, in particular, when statements of the eye-witnesses were in line with their statements recorded under Section 161 Cr.P.C. The witnesses including the injured witnesses remained firm and consistent qua the date, time, place mode and manner of the occurrence. They were cross-examined at length but the prosecution has failed to extract any favourable material from their mouths. 15. The case of the prosecution consists upon three tiers. Appellant Muhammad Saleem Shehzad @ Hamza was named in the crime report with the specific role of causing fire arm injuries to the deceased and the injured witnesses with his pistol 9 MM. Appellant Waqas and Abu Ammar were not named in the crime report, who were implicated in this case through supplementary statement of the complainant. Respondents No.1 & 3 Shehbaz @ Tipu and Yousaf Ameen were also nominated in the crime report but they have been acquitted of the charge by the trial Court. First of all, we would like to take up the case of appellant Muhammad Saleem Shehzad @ Hamza. He was named in the crime report with the role that in response to the Lalkara raised by his co-accused, he stated that he had already managed to teach lesson to the complainant party and made fire shots with his pistol 9-MM hitting at the chest, head and flank of deceased Ali Raza resulting into his death and a grazed wound through the eye bros of injured Kamran Shafique (PW-7).

First of all, we would like to take up the case of appellant Muhammad Saleem Shehzad @ Hamza. He was named in the crime report with the role that in response to the Lalkara raised by his co-accused, he stated that he had already managed to teach lesson to the complainant party and made fire shots with his pistol 9-MM hitting at the chest, head and flank of deceased Ali Raza resulting into his death and a grazed wound through the eye bros of injured Kamran Shafique (PW-7). Dr. Riasat Ali (PW-14) held autopsy on the dead body of deceased Ali Raza and confirmed the availability of fire Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 10 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 arm injuries attributed to this appellant, which were found fatal to his life. Dr. Tanveer Hussain (PW-15) conducted medico-legal examination of the said injured witness and confirmed the availability of aforesaid injury attracting the provisions of Section 337-A(i) PPC. The injured witness as well as the medical expert remained firm and consistent qua their respective statements in cross-examination conducted by the defence at considerable length. The medical evidence lends full support to the ocular account. Though the recovery of weapon of offence from this appellant remained inconsequential due to the reason that the crime empties were dispatched to the office of Forensic Expert on 04.04.2014 after his arrest yet the same being corroboratory piece of evidence, alone is not sufficient to discard the confidence inspiring ocular account to his extent. We are persuaded to hold that the prosecution has succeeded to prove the charge against this appellant beyond shadow of reasonable doubt and as such we found no legitimate exception to differ with the conclusion arrived at by the trial Court qua his conviction. 16. So far as the role of appellants Abu Ammar Yasir and Waqas @ Baban is concerned, admittedly they are not nominated in the crime report. They were implicated in this case by the complainant through his supplementary statement on 28.03.2014 but without disclosing any source of information for their implication and participation in this occurrence.

So far as the role of appellants Abu Ammar Yasir and Waqas @ Baban is concerned, admittedly they are not nominated in the crime report. They were implicated in this case by the complainant through his supplementary statement on 28.03.2014 but without disclosing any source of information for their implication and participation in this occurrence. Although, in cross-examination the complainant stated that names of the aforesaid appellants were told to him by the injured witnesses and this fact was also endorsed by one of the injured witnesses yet the question remains that the said injured witness also did not disclose the source of his information. Had any of the injured witnesses knew any of the unknown accused, he should have incorporated his/their name in the statement recorded under Section 161 Cr.P.C. Even our query to the learned counsel for the complainant in this regard, remained un-attended. Furthermore, the scaled site plan (Ex.PCC) was prepared on the day when the complainant got recorded his supplementary statement implicating these accused in this case and if names of these appellants were known to him on that very day, he should have certainly got incorporated their names in the scaled site plan Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 11 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 which is still showing “unknown accused”, which negates the claim of the complainant. These appellants were identified during test identification parade conducted on 05.04.2014 under the supervision of Mr. Muhammad Akram Azad, Magistrate (PW-13), which is the only positive piece of evidence available with the prosecution against these appellants but we have observed that the said test identification parade was defective for the following reasons:- Firstly, no facial feature of the unknown accused was given either in the crime report or in the statements of eye witnesses recorded under Sections 161 Cr.P.C. and as such the test identification parade in the absence of such features has no sanctity in the eye of law in view of the dictum laid down in case titled “SABIR ALI alias FAUJI versus The STATE” (2011 SCMR 563) wherein it has been held as under:- “It is also settled principle that identification test is of no value when description/feature of accused is not given in the contents of the F.I.R.” Secondly, both these appellants were nominated by the complainant on the day one in the supplementary statement with their parentage and address and in such backdrop, the exercise of test identification parade was nothing but a futile exercise.

These appellants were identified during test identification parade conducted on 05.04.2014 under the supervision of Mr. Muhammad Akram Azad, Magistrate (PW-13), which is the only positive piece of evidence available with the prosecution against these appellants but we have observed that the said test identification parade was defective for the following reasons:- Firstly, no facial feature of the unknown accused was given either in the crime report or in the statements of eye witnesses recorded under Sections 161 Cr.P.C. and as such the test identification parade in the absence of such features has no sanctity in the eye of law in view of the dictum laid down in case titled “SABIR ALI alias FAUJI versus The STATE” (2011 SCMR 563) wherein it has been held as under:- “It is also settled principle that identification test is of no value when description/feature of accused is not given in the contents of the F.I.R.” Secondly, both these appellants were nominated by the complainant on the day one in the supplementary statement with their parentage and address and in such backdrop, the exercise of test identification parade was nothing but a futile exercise. In case reported as Muhammad Yaqoob and another versus The State (1989 P.Cr.L.J 2227), the learned Division Bench of this Court has observed as under:- “The identification parades ( as they are normally called) are necessary only where the offender was a complete stranger to the witnesses. Ismail and another v. The State 1974 S C M R 175. And the whole object of the identification proceedings is to find out whether the suspect was or was not the real offender Satya Narain v. The State AIR 1953 All.385 and Kind v.Christle 1914 AC 545.” The above laid down principle has recently been approved by the Hon’ble Apex Court in case reported as “Kanwar Anwaar Ali, Special Judicial Magistrate, in the matter of Criminal Miscellaneous Application No.183 of 2019 in Criminal Appeal No.259 of 2018” (PLD 2019 SC 488). Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 12 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 Thirdly, appellant Abu Ammar Rashid was identified by the complainant alone while appellant Waqas @ Baban was identified by the complainant as well as the injured witness but with a joint role of making fire shots at the deceased and the injured witnesses, which is completely in disregard to their statements earlier recorded under Section 161 Cr.P.C. wherein specific role of making fire shots to each individual was assigned by the injured witnesses. The evidentiary value of test identification parade without assigning specific role, is next to nothing. We are not in agreement with the conclusion arrived at by the trial Court qua conviction and sentence of these appellants. 17. As far as the case of respondents No.1 & 3/acquitted accused is concerned, we have observed that in the crime report, respondent No.1/Shehbaz @ Tipu was assigned the role of making ineffective firing.

We are not in agreement with the conclusion arrived at by the trial Court qua conviction and sentence of these appellants. 17. As far as the case of respondents No.1 & 3/acquitted accused is concerned, we have observed that in the crime report, respondent No.1/Shehbaz @ Tipu was assigned the role of making ineffective firing. He was resident of the house where the complainant party visited for compromise. Admittedly, the occurrence had taken place near the Chowk where none else had claimed to have sustained fire arm injuries at the hands of this respondent. In this unfortunate incident, five persons lost their lives while three others sustained fire arm injuries but not even a single deceased or injured witnesses sustained any injury at the hands of this respondent, which creates reasonable doubt regarding his involvement and participation in this occurrence. Respondent No.3/Yousaf Ameen was attributed the role of causing fire arm injury to deceased Muhammad Rafaqat. Muhammad Kashif (PW-6) deposed that “Yousaf Ameen accused made fire shots at Rafaqat, which hit on his right flank again said on left flank.” which is belied by the medical evidence. According to the post mortem report of said deceased, injury at right flank was non-existent, however, there was an entry wound at right upper chest adjacent to shoulder and exited through left flank. In this view of the matter, the deceased had no entry wound at right or left flank as suggested by the eye witnesses. For the foregoing reasons, the trial Court had rightly extended the benefit of doubt to these respondents. Upon our own independent evaluation of the evidence available on record, we are fully convinced that the prosecution has failed to prove the charge against these respondents beyond shadow of reasonable doubt. Even otherwise, Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 13 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 after acquittal an accused earns the double presumption of innocence, which can only be interfered if the order/judgment impugned is found to be perverse, arbitrary, capricious or patently illegal.

Even otherwise, Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 13 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 after acquittal an accused earns the double presumption of innocence, which can only be interfered if the order/judgment impugned is found to be perverse, arbitrary, capricious or patently illegal. The judgment impugned herein does not fall under any of the said parameters and thus, to the extent of respondents it warrants no interference by this Court. 18. The post mortem examination on the dead bodies of deceased Muhammad Umer, Muhammad Akram, Muhammad Rafaqat, Muhammad Raza and Jehangir was conducted on 26.04.2014 wherein the respective medical officers observed fire arm wounds at the chest of deceased Muhammad Umer, Muhammad Akram, Muhammad Rafaqat, Muhammad Raza in addition to three injuries at the dead body of deceased Muhammad Rafaqat, whereas, three fire arm injuries were found on the dead body of deceased accused Jehangir. The cause of death of each deceased was injuries at their chest caused by fire arm, which were anti mortem in nature and sufficient to cause death in ordinary course of nature. The probable duration between injuries and death was immediate whereas between death and postmortem examination within 14-15 hours. Dr. Tanvir (PW-15) medically examined injured PWs Kamran Shafique and Kashif on 25.03.2014 and confirmed fire arm injuries on their persons. The purpose of medical evidence is always to ascertain the cause of death, nature, number and duration of injuries and the kind of weapon used for causing such injury but it alone does not raise accusing finger towards any specific accused. The medical evidence, as discussed in the preceding paragraph, lends full support to the ocular account furnished to the extent of appellant Saleem Shahzad @ Hamza for causing fire arm injuries to deceased Ali Raza and injured Kamran Shafique. 19. The motive as set up by the prosecution was a dispute of a house occupied by sister of the complainant.

The medical evidence, as discussed in the preceding paragraph, lends full support to the ocular account furnished to the extent of appellant Saleem Shahzad @ Hamza for causing fire arm injuries to deceased Ali Raza and injured Kamran Shafique. 19. The motive as set up by the prosecution was a dispute of a house occupied by sister of the complainant. It was in the name of her father-in- law but her “Nand” namely, Mst. Saira, who had contracted love marriage with Jahangir, had fraudulently got transferred it in her name for which father-in-law of the complainant’s sister obtained injunctive order from civil Court and on the day of occurrence Mst. Saira alongwith her co-accused came to take possession of the said house, which resulted into this Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 14 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 unfortunate incident. The motive was never denied by the defence rather they produced the documents showing that the house had been transferred in the name of Mst. Saira. The transfer of house in her name does not give license to any individual to get vacate the house by taking the law in hand. The motive as set up by the prosecution has fully been endorsed by the defence itself. 20. The recovery of gun pash-pash and rifle 222-bore was shown against appellants Abu Ammar Rashid and Waqas respectively. It has been observed that the recovery of weapon of offence from appellant Abu Ammar Rashid remained inconsequential as the crime empties secured from the place of occurrence were dispatched to the office of Ballistic Expert after his arrest and the possibility cannot be ruled out of consideration that the same had been maneuvered after making fire shot from the weapon shown to have been recovered from his possession. A reference in this regard may be placed to case titled “NAZEER AHMED versus The STATE” (2016 SCMR 1628) wherein it has been held as under:- “the crime-empty secured from the place of occurrence was sent to the Forensic Science Laboratory after recovery of the gun rendering such recovery to be legally unacceptable------” Though the report of Ballistic Expert is positive one to the extent of appellant Waqas yet the same being corroboratory piece of evidence alone is not sufficient to maintain the conviction and sentence of the said appellant when the ocular account to his extent is neither convincing nor confidence inspiring. 21.

It has been observed that the recovery of weapon of offence from appellant Abu Ammar Rashid remained inconsequential as the crime empties secured from the place of occurrence were dispatched to the office of Ballistic Expert after his arrest and the possibility cannot be ruled out of consideration that the same had been maneuvered after making fire shot from the weapon shown to have been recovered from his possession. A reference in this regard may be placed to case titled “NAZEER AHMED versus The STATE” (2016 SCMR 1628) wherein it has been held as under:- “the crime-empty secured from the place of occurrence was sent to the Forensic Science Laboratory after recovery of the gun rendering such recovery to be legally unacceptable------” Though the report of Ballistic Expert is positive one to the extent of appellant Waqas yet the same being corroboratory piece of evidence alone is not sufficient to maintain the conviction and sentence of the said appellant when the ocular account to his extent is neither convincing nor confidence inspiring. 21. Admittedly, this incident had taken place near the Chowk on a dispute over the possession of a residential house between sister of the complainant and in-laws of her “Nand” where the complainant party had gone to patch up the matter. This, by itself, indicates that the act of the assailants was the result of a private vendetta with no design or purpose as contemplated in by Section 6(b) or (c) of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997. The obvious facts are coherent with the facts of case titled ‘KHUDA-E-NOOR versus The STATE’ (PLD 2016 SC 195) wherein it has been held that:- Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 15 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 “The provisions section 6 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 which define “terrorism” clearly show that the said section is divided into two main parts i.e. the first part contained in section (b) and (c) of the said Act dealing with the mens rea mentioning the “design’ or the “purpose” behind an action and the second part falling in section 6 of the said Act specifying the actions which, if coupled with the mens rea mentioned above, would constitute the offence of “terrorism”. ------ The case in hand was a case of a private motive set up in the FIR and during the trial the motive set up in the FIR was changed by the prosecution and an element of honour killing was introduced but even that did not change the character of the offence which was nothing but a private offence committed in the privacy of a home with no design or purpose contemplated by section (b) or (c) of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997. 22. Gathering all these facts and circumstances of the case, we have reached at an irresistible conclusion that the prosecution has been able to prove the charge against appellant Saleem Shehzad, who was specifically named in the crime report with the specific role of causing fire arm injuries to deceased Ali Raza and injured witness namely, Kamran Shafique, which were fully endorsed by the medical evidence.

Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 15 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 “The provisions section 6 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 which define “terrorism” clearly show that the said section is divided into two main parts i.e. the first part contained in section (b) and (c) of the said Act dealing with the mens rea mentioning the “design’ or the “purpose” behind an action and the second part falling in section 6 of the said Act specifying the actions which, if coupled with the mens rea mentioned above, would constitute the offence of “terrorism”. ------ The case in hand was a case of a private motive set up in the FIR and during the trial the motive set up in the FIR was changed by the prosecution and an element of honour killing was introduced but even that did not change the character of the offence which was nothing but a private offence committed in the privacy of a home with no design or purpose contemplated by section (b) or (c) of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997. 22. Gathering all these facts and circumstances of the case, we have reached at an irresistible conclusion that the prosecution has been able to prove the charge against appellant Saleem Shehzad, who was specifically named in the crime report with the specific role of causing fire arm injuries to deceased Ali Raza and injured witness namely, Kamran Shafique, which were fully endorsed by the medical evidence. We have no legitimate exception to differ with the conclusion arrived at by the trial court qua the conviction of appellant Saleem Shehzad in offence under Sections 302(b) PPC. So far as the quantum of sentence of the said appellant is concerned, we have observed that the motive behind the occurrence was not directly attributed to him, the recovery of weapon of offence was inconsequential and some of the co-accused, who had participated during the occurrence, were acquitted of the charge by the trial Court, which are sufficient to award lesser sentence. A Reference may be made to case titled “ABDUL NABI versus The STATE” (2017 SCMR 335):- “Apart from that a co-accused of the appellant namely Ghulam Yasin, attributed a role identical to that alleged against the appellant, has already been acquitted by the trial court and, thus, some doubts about the veracity of the prosecution’s case have emerged which doubts about the veracity of the prosecution’s case having the same may well be considered towards exercising caution in the matter of the appellant’s sentence of death.” It is also settled principle of law that when a case qualifies the awarding of both sentences of imprisonment for life and that of the death, the proper course for the Courts, as a matter of caution, is to give preference to the Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 16 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 lesser sentence. Reference may be made to case titled “GHULAM MOHY- UD-DIN alias HAJI BABU and others versus The STATE” (2014 SCMR 1034) wherein it has been observed at page 1044 as:- “In any case, if a single doubt or ground is available, creating reasonable doubt in the mind of Court/Judge to award death penalty or life imprisonment, it would be sufficient circumstances to adopt alternative course by awarding life imprisonment instead of death sentence.” 23.

Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 16 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 lesser sentence. Reference may be made to case titled “GHULAM MOHY- UD-DIN alias HAJI BABU and others versus The STATE” (2014 SCMR 1034) wherein it has been observed at page 1044 as:- “In any case, if a single doubt or ground is available, creating reasonable doubt in the mind of Court/Judge to award death penalty or life imprisonment, it would be sufficient circumstances to adopt alternative course by awarding life imprisonment instead of death sentence.” 23. The prosecution has miserably failed to bring home guilt of appellants Abu Ammar Rashid and Waqas @ Babban beyond shadow of reasonable doubt. They were booked in this case through supplementary statement by the complainant without disclosing the source of his information. The facial features of the unknown accused were also not described by the complainant and the injured witnesses in their statements recorded under Section 154 & 161 Cr.P.C. The identification of these appellants during test identification parade after nominating them in the supplementary statement and that too with joint role is not free from doubt. It is well settled that even a slightest doubt is sufficient to tilt the scale of justice in favour of the accused. Furthermore, the reasons rendered by the trial Court qua acquittal of respondents Shehbaz and Yousaf Ameen for extending them the benefit of doubt are free from any illegality or perversity, which does not warrant interference by this Court. 24. In view of what has been discussed above, Criminal Appeal No.1914 of 2016 is partly allowed and appellant Saleem Shehzad @ Hamza is acquitted of the charge in offences under Sections 7(a), 7(b) & 7(c) of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 and Section 337-F(iii) PPC. The conviction of the said appellant in offence under Section 302(b) PPC is hereby maintained, however his sentence of capital punishment is converted into the one of imprisonment for life. The amount of compensation and the sentence in lieu thereof shall remain intact. The conviction and sentence of the said appellant in offences under Sections 324, 337-A(i) PPC shall remain intact.

The amount of compensation and the sentence in lieu thereof shall remain intact. The conviction and sentence of the said appellant in offences under Sections 324, 337-A(i) PPC shall remain intact. The benefit of Section 382-B Cr.P.C. is extended to him. This appeal stands allowed to the extent of appellants Abu Ammar Rashid and Waqas @ Babban and they are acquitted of the charge by extending the benefit of Crl. Appeal No.1914 of 2016 CSR No.21-T of 2016 Crl.Appeal No.2102 of 2010 17 Crl.Revision No.1998 of 2016 doubt to them. They are directed to be released from jail forthwith, if not required to be detained in any other criminal case. 25. Capital Sentence Reference No.21-T of 2016 is answered in negative and death sentence of appellants Abu Ammar Rashid, Waqas and Saleem Shehzad @ Hamza is NOT confirmed. Crl. Appeal No.2102 of 2016 & Crl. Revision No.1998 of 2016 26. For the reasons recorded hereinabove, both these matters are without any merit, the same stand dismissed.
